title: 10 best herbal capsules for heart pain
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for heart pain are dietary supplements that contain a variety of natural ingredients, such as hawthorn, motherwort, and turmeric, which have been traditionally used to support heart health. These herbs work by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and strengthening the cardiovascular system. For example, hawthorn has been shown to dilate coronary arteries, increasing blood flow to the heart, while turmeric contains curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the risk of heart disease. By taking herbal capsules for heart pain, individuals can improve their quality of life by reducing symptoms such as chest pain, shortness of breath, and fatigue. Furthermore, these supplements can provide a natural and holistic approach to heart health, without the potential side effects of prescription medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Midland hawthorn capsule helps with heart pain because it contains active compounds that improve blood flow to the heart, increasing the pumping capacity of the heart and reducing its workload. The herb has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various heart conditions, including angina and hypertension. Midland hawthorn contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, it contains proanthocyanidins, which help dilate coronary arteries and improve the overall health of the cardiovascular system. These properties make midland hawthorn an effective remedy for heart pain, providing relief and promoting heart health.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic: Midland hawthorn capsules help strengthen and regulate the heart muscle, improving its ability to pump blood and alleviating heart pain, due to the presence of flavonoids and procyanidins.

Anti-ischemic: The herb improves blood flow to the heart, reducing ischemia-induced heart pain, by increasing the bioavailability of nitric oxide through its active constituents, including oligomeric procyanidins.

Anti-arrhythmic: Midland hawthorn capsules help maintain a normal heart rhythm, reducing heart pain caused by arrhythmias, due to the presence of various cardioactive compounds, such as rutin and hyperoside.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of midland hawthorn protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to heart pain, thanks to its rich content of polyphenols, including flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.

Anti-inflammatory: Midland hawthorn capsules reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, alleviating heart pain, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which is attributed to the herb's bioactive compounds, such as triterpene acids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the midland hawthorn plant contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing heart pain and improving overall cardiovascular health.
Flowers: The flowers of the midland hawthorn plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and relax blood vessels, thereby reducing heart pain and promoting heart health.

Berries: The berries of the midland hawthorn plant contain high levels of antioxidants, which help protect the heart from damage and improve overall cardiovascular health, thereby reducing heart pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the midland hawthorn plant contain compounds that help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease and alleviating heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1,000 mg of dried Midland Hawthorn berries and grind them into a fine powder.
Measure out 500 mg of the powder and mix it with an appropriate binder, such as microcrystalline cellulose, to create a homogeneous mixture.
Fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry completely before storing them in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take one capsule, twice daily, or as directed by a healthcare professional, for the treatment of heart pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common hawthorn capsule helps with heart pain because it contains active compounds that can dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and protect against damage to blood vessels. These properties can help reduce the workload on the heart and alleviate symptoms of heart pain or angina. Additionally, hawthorn has been shown to have a positive effect on heart rhythm and can help regulate heartbeat, further supporting heart health. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may also contribute to its cardioprotective benefits. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking hawthorn or any other supplement for heart-related conditions.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic: Hawthorn capsules can help strengthen and normalize the heart's function, which is crucial for alleviating heart pain due to their high content of flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.
Vasodilatory: Hawthorn can dilate coronary vessels, improving blood flow to the heart, thus reducing heart pain, owing to the presence of compounds like epicatechin and rutin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of hawthorn help protect heart tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which is important for heart pain management as it may reduce the risk of heart disease, attributed to the high levels of oligomeric procyanidins (OPCs).

Anti-inflammatory: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, alleviating heart pain by decreasing the risk of heart-related conditions, associated with the flavonoids and saponins found in hawthorn.

Negative Inotropic: Hawthorn has a negative inotropic effect, which means it can help regulate heartbeat and reduce heart palpitations, thereby alleviating heart pain, due to the presence of active compounds like crataegus laevigata and vitexin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Hawthorn flowers are rich in antioxidants and can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and alleviating heart pain.
Leaves: Hawthorn leaves contain flavonoids that have a positive inotropic effect, strengthening heart contractions and reducing heart pain.
Berries: Hawthorn berries and seeds are used to make medicinal capsules for heart pain due to their high concentration of anthocyanidins and procyanidins, which improve cardiovascular health.
Bark: Hawthorn bark is a source of triterpene acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce heart pain.
Stems: Hawthorn stems contain tannins that have astringent properties, which can help improve heart health and alleviate heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried common hawthorn berries, crush them, and mix with 200mg of empty vegetarian capsules.
In a dry blender, grind the crushed berries into a fine powder for approximately 2-3 minutes.
Fill each capsule with the hawthorn powder, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, ensuring consistent dosage.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry place, for up to six months.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, to alleviate heart pain, following the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove capsule helps with heart pain because it contains digoxin, a substance that can help improve heart function. Digoxin helps the heart muscle contract more forcefully, which can increase blood circulation and reduce heart pain. Additionally, foxglove can help regulate heart rhythms, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing irregular heartbeats or arrhythmias. By promoting a stronger, more stable heartbeat, foxglove capsules can help alleviate heart pain and improve overall cardiovascular health. However, it's important to note that foxglove should only be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can interact with other medications and may cause adverse effects in some individuals.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic: Foxglove capsules contain cardiac glycosides, such as digoxin and digitoxin, which increase the force of heart contractions and improve heart function, thereby alleviating heart pain.
Negative inotrope: The cardiac glycosides in foxglove capsules reduce heart rate and oxygen demand, which can help manage heart pain caused by angina or arrhythmias.
Diuretic: Foxglove capsules help eliminate excess fluid from the body, reducing the workload on the heart and alleviating heart pain associated with heart failure or hypertension.
Vasodilatory: The cardiac glycosides in foxglove capsules dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the heart, which can help alleviate heart pain.

Antiarrhythmic: Foxglove capsules can help regulate heart rhythm, reducing the occurrence of irregular heartbeats and associated heart pain. This property is attributed to the cardiac glycosides, which help maintain a normal heart rhythm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Foxglove flowers contain digoxin and other cardiac glycosides, which help regulate the heartbeat and alleviate heart pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the foxglove plant are also rich in cardiac glycosides, making them useful for treating heart pain and irregular heartbeats.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, foxglove seeds contain trace amounts of cardiac glycosides and can be used in the production of medicinal capsules for heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh foxglove leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Thoroughly rinse the foxglove parts, then chop them into small pieces and let them dry for 24-48 hours in a cool, dark place.
Grind the dried foxglove pieces into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric coffee grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the foxglove powder with 1 teaspoon of a filler such as rice flour or cornstarch.
Fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the foxglove-filler mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand. Store in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peruvian bark capsule helps with heart pain because it contains a potent active ingredient called quinine, which has been used traditionally to treat heart-related conditions. Quinine works by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, thereby alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with heart-related ailments. Moreover, it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and prevent the formation of blood clots, further promoting cardiovascular health. By addressing the root causes of heart pain, Peruvian bark capsules offer a natural and effective solution for managing heart-related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peruvian bark contains anti-inflammatory alkaloids, such as taspine and lunacridine, which help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating heart pain.
Vasodilatory: The bark contains vasodilatory compounds, including quinoline alkaloids, that widen blood vessels, improving blood flow, and reducing the strain on the heart, thus easing heart pain.

Anti-arrhythmic: Peruvian bark has anti-arrhythmic properties due to its alkaloid content, particularly the presence of quinoline alkaloids, which help regulate heart rhythm and prevent irregular heartbeats, thereby alleviating heart pain.

Antioxidant: The bark possesses antioxidant properties, attributed to its alkaloids and phenolic compounds, that help protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage, thereby promoting heart health and reducing heart pain.

Platelet aggregation inhibition: Peruvian bark inhibits platelet aggregation, thanks to its alkaloids such as quinine and quinidine, reducing the risk of blood clots and improving blood flow, thereby alleviating heart pain.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Peruvian bark plant contains high levels of alkaloids, such as cinchona, which have been shown to be effective in treating heart pain and regulating heartbeat.
Roots: The roots of the Peruvian bark plant contain similar alkaloids as the bark, making them useful in the production of medicinal capsules for heart pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the Peruvian bark plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them helpful in reducing heart pain and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the Peruvian bark plant contain essential oils and alkaloids that have been shown to have a positive effect on heart health and the regulation of heart rhythm.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Peruvian bark, 1/2 teaspoon of cinnamon powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of powdered ginger root.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix thoroughly.
Add enough gelatin capsule contents to make a thick paste, about 1-2 teaspoons.
Fill empty gelatin capsule halves with the herbal paste and join together to form a sealed capsule.
Let the capsules dry for 24 hours before storing in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsule helps with heart pain because it contains allicin, a compound that has been shown to help reduce the risk of heart disease. Allicin works by relaxing blood vessels, improving blood flow, and reducing cholesterol levels. This, in turn, can help to alleviate heart pain by reducing the strain on the heart and improving overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the arteries, further reducing the risk of heart pain. Overall, taking a garlic capsule can be a natural and effective way to support heart health and alleviate heart pain.
remedy_properties: Antiplatelet aggregation: Garlic inhibits the clumping together of platelets in the blood, reducing the risk of blood clots that can cause heart pain and damage to the heart muscle. This property is due to the active constituents ajoene and allicin.

Antioxidant: Garlic has antioxidant properties that protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress, which can contribute to heart pain and other heart diseases. The antioxidant properties come from various organosulfur compounds found in garlic.

Hypolipidemic: Garlic helps lower levels of total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, and triglycerides in the blood, reducing the risk of heart pain and heart disease. The hypolipidemic effect is attributed to the active constituent allicin.

Vasodilation: Garlic has vasodilatory effects, relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, which can help alleviate heart pain and reduce the risk of heart disease. The vasodilatory properties are due to the active constituents nitric oxide and hydrogen sulfide.

Anti-inflammatory: Garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to heart pain and other heart diseases. The anti-inflammatory effects are due to the active constituents diallyl disulfide and S-allylcysteine.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: Garlic bulbs are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for heart pain due to their rich content of allicin, a compound known for its cardioprotective properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the garlic plant have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate heart pain, as they contain beneficial compounds like allicin and diallyl trisulfide that support heart health.
Flowers: Garlic flowers contain valuable bioactive compounds that can help reduce heart pain by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and lowering blood pressure.

Seeds: Garlic seeds are a lesser-known source of medicinal compounds, but they contain beneficial compounds like allicin and alliin, which can help alleviate heart pain by promoting cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Crush 6 cloves of garlic and mix with 2 tablespoons of organic olive oil, let it sit for 10 minutes to activate the allicin.
In a dry blender, combine 1 cup of dried hawthorn berries, 1/2 cup of dried motherwort leaves, and the garlic-olive oil mixture, blend until a powdery consistency is achieved.
Add 1/2 cup of powdered brown rice as a filler and blend again until well combined.
Using a capsule filling machine, fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the herbal garlic mixture, ensuring each capsule contains approximately 500mg of the blend.
Store the finished herbal garlic capsules in a cool, dry, and dark place, and take 2 capsules daily or as directed by a healthcare professional for heart pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsule helps with heart pain because it contains salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation and pain in the heart, making it an effective natural remedy for heart-related conditions such as angina. Additionally, white willow has been shown to have a positive effect on blood circulation, which can help improve heart health and reduce the risk of heart-related complications. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before taking white willow capsules, especially if you have a pre-existing heart condition or are taking other medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White willow capsules contain salicin, which is converted to salicylic acid in the body, reducing inflammation and alleviating pain associated with heart conditions.
Analgesic: Salicin, the active constituent, has pain-relieving properties that help manage heart pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that cause pain and inflammation.
Antipyretic: White willow has fever-reducing abilities, which can help regulate body temperature and reduce stress on the cardiovascular system during heart-related issues.
Blood vessel dilation: The active compounds in white willow, such as flavonoids, can help dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and oxygen supply to the heart, thereby alleviating heart pain.
Antithrombotic: White willow may help prevent blood clots, reducing the risk of heart attacks and strokes, which are often associated with heart pain.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow tree is commonly used in medicinal capsules for heart pain due to its high salicin content, which acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and pain reliever.
Leaves: White willow leaves are sometimes included in medicinal capsules for heart pain because they contain salicin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Stems: Although less potent than the bark, white willow stems also contain salicin and may be used in medicinal capsules to help treat heart pain and inflammation.

Rhizomes: White willow rhizomes, the underground stems of the plant, are occasionally used in medicinal capsules for heart pain due to their salicin content, which can help reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 gram of dried white willow bark and 240 milligrams of hawthorn berries, two evidence-based herbs for heart health.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herbs together and fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture.
Store the finished capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, especially for heart-related conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterfly weed capsule helps with heart pain because it contains a powerful compound called "calotropegin" that has been shown to dilate coronary arteries, thereby improving blood flow to the heart. This herbal remedy has been traditionally used to treat various heart ailments, including angina and arrhythmia. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of butterfly weed also help alleviate pain and inflammation in the heart, promoting overall cardiovascular health. Moreover, butterfly weed is rich in antioxidants that protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage, further reducing the risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butterfly weed contains compounds such as alkaloids and cardiac glycosides that help reduce inflammation, which is important for heart pain as inflammation can worsen heart conditions.
Cardiotonic: Butterfly weed has cardiotonic properties due to its cardiac glycosides, which help strengthen the heart muscle and improve its overall function, alleviating heart pain.
Vasodilatory: The plant contains alkaloids that act as vasodilators, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the strain on the heart and alleviating pain.
Antispasmodic: Butterfly weed has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax smooth muscles and alleviate spasms, thereby reducing heart pain caused by muscle contractions.
Analgesic: Butterfly weed contains analgesic compounds, which help to reduce pain and discomfort, providing relief for heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve heart health.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that can help protect the heart from damage and improve cardiovascular function.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that can help lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease.
Leaves: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate heart pain and improve overall heart health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 leaves from a butterfly weed plant, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests or diseases.
Dry the leaves in a cool, dark place for 1-2 weeks, until they become crisp and crumble easily.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the butterfly weed powder with 1 teaspoon of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to create a paste.
Fill size 000 empty gelatin capsules with the paste, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, and store in a cool, dry place. Take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily as needed for heart pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European mistletoe capsule helps with heart pain because it contains active compounds that can dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and regulate heartbeat. This herbal remedy has been used traditionally to treat various cardiovascular conditions, including angina (chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart). Mistletoe extract can help reduce the oxygen demand of the heart muscle, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort. Furthermore, it may have a positive impact on the heart's electrical activity, contributing to its potential therapeutic effects on heart-related issues.
remedy_properties: Antiarrhythmic property: European mistletoe helps regulate heartbeat and reduce irregular heart rhythms, which is important for heart pain as it can alleviate symptoms and potentially prevent heart-related complications. The active constituents responsible for this property include various triterpene acids, such as oleanolic and ursolic acids.

Vasodilatory property: European mistletoe helps widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which is crucial for heart pain as it can help alleviate chest pain and reduce the risk of heart attacks. The active constituents responsible for this property include mistletoe lectins and viscosides.

Antioxidant property: European mistletoe has antioxidant effects that protect heart cells from oxidative stress and damage, which is important for heart pain as it can help prevent further heart damage and promote recovery. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin.

Anti-inflammatory property: European mistletoe has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which is important for heart pain as it can alleviate symptoms and potentially prevent heart disease progression. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpene acids and lectins.

Cardioprotective property: European mistletoe has been shown to protect the heart from various forms of damage, including ischemia-reperfusion injury, which is important for heart pain as it can help prevent and treat heart disease. The active constituents responsible for this property include various compounds, such as viscosides, lectins, and triterpene acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including viscosides, which have been shown to dilate coronary vessels and improve blood flow to the heart.
Stems: Contain similar compounds to the leaves, although in lower concentrations, and can help support the cardiovascular system.
Berries: While not typically used in medicinal capsules, the berries contain antioxidants that can help protect the heart from damage.
Bark: Contains unique compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve heart function.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh European mistletoe (Viscum album) branches, ensuring they are harvested sustainably and legally.
Thoroughly clean the mistletoe, then chop it into small pieces and dry them in a dark, well-ventilated area for 3-5 days.
Grind the dried mistletoe pieces into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 200-300mg of the mistletoe powder and encapsulate it in size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry, dark place and take 1-2 capsules daily, as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arjuna capsule helps with heart pain because it contains the extract of the bark of the Arjuna tree, which is known for its cardiovascular benefits. Arjuna is rich in coenzyme Q10, minerals, and antioxidants that help strengthen the heart muscles, improve blood circulation, and lower blood pressure. It also helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to heart pain. Additionally, Arjuna has been shown to improve the heart's ability to pump blood, reducing the workload on the heart and alleviating pain. Overall, Arjuna capsule provides a natural and effective solution for managing heart pain and promoting heart health.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective: Arjuna capsules contain compounds such as tannins and saponins (e.g., arjunetosides) that help protect the heart from damage and improve its functioning, which is crucial for alleviating heart pain.

Anti-inflammatory: The herb contains active constituents like lupeol and arjunic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, helping reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating heart pain.
Antioxidant: Arjuna is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, that help neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, promoting heart health and reducing heart pain.
Hypolipidemic: Arjuna capsules have been shown to lower lipid levels, reducing the risk of atherosclerosis and improving blood flow, thus helping manage heart pain caused by poor circulation.
Vasodilatory: Arjuna contains compounds like arjunolic acid and tannins, which help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the heart, thereby alleviating heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds such as triterpene saponins, which have been shown to have a positive effect on heart health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the bark of the arjuna plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat heart-related ailments.
Seeds: Contain high levels of coenzyme Q10, a nutrient that plays a crucial role in heart health by protecting cells from damage and improving energy production in cells.
Leaves: High in flavonoids and other antioxidants, the leaves of the arjuna plant have been shown to have a positive effect on heart health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried Arjuna bark into a fine powder, typically taking 30 minutes.
Mix the Arjuna powder with 100g of filler material, such as microcrystalline cellulose, and pass through a 40-mesh sieve for uniformity.
Measure 500mg of the blended powder per capsule, using a capsule filling machine to ensure consistent dosage.
Fill and seal empty gelatin capsules with the measured powder, a process taking about 20 minutes for 100 capsules.
Store the finished Arjuna capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, for a shelf life of up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea capsule helps with heart pain because it contains high levels of antioxidants, particularly flavonoids, which have been shown to help reduce the risk of heart disease. These antioxidants work to improve blood vessel function, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure, all of which can contribute to heart pain. Additionally, white tea has been found to help reduce levels of bad cholesterol and increase good cholesterol, further supporting heart health. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate pain and discomfort in the heart. Overall, white tea capsules can be a valuable addition to a heart-healthy lifestyle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce heart pain by decreasing inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thanks to its high concentration of flavonoids and polyphenols.

Antioxidant: White tea's powerful antioxidants protect the heart by neutralizing free radicals, which can damage heart tissue and contribute to heart pain, due to the presence of catechins and other polyphenols.

Vasodilation: White tea helps alleviate heart pain by promoting vasodilation, which improves blood flow and reduces the strain on the heart, attributable to its rich content of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and theaflavins.

Anti-atherosclerotic: White tea's anti-atherosclerotic properties help prevent heart pain by inhibiting the formation of plaque in the arteries, reducing the risk of heart attacks and strokes, owing to its abundance of catechins and flavonoids.

Lowering blood pressure: White tea helps manage heart pain by reducing high blood pressure, as it relaxes blood vessels and improves endothelial function, which is facilitated by its active constituents, such as epicatechins and thearubigins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help improve heart health and alleviate heart pain.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce the risk of heart disease and provide relief from heart pain.
Stems: Contain catechins, which have been found to improve blood flow and reduce the risk of heart-related issues, thus helping to alleviate heart pain.
Flowers: Rich in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce heart pain and promote overall heart health.
Roots: Contain phenolic acids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on heart health, thereby reducing heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried hawthorn berries, 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm, and 1 teaspoon of dried motherwort – all known for their heart-healthy benefits.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, for approximately 5 minutes.
Mix the ground herbs with 1 teaspoon of pure, uncoated white tea powder, which is rich in antioxidants.
Add the herbal mixture to empty vegetarian capsules, using a capsule-filling machine or manually, filling each side of the capsule evenly.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months, taking 1-2 capsules daily as needed for heart pain relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat heart pain with herbal capsules is by using a combination of natural ingredients known to support heart health. Hawthorn berry, for example, is widely recognized for its ability to strengthen the heart muscle and improve blood flow. Additionally, Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10) is a powerful antioxidant that can help reduce inflammation and improve energy production in the heart. Another beneficial herb is turmeric, which contains curcumin, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce heart-related inflammation. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heart pain that are treated with herbal capsules include angina and hypertension. Angina is chest pain or discomfort caused by an inadequate supply of oxygen-rich blood to your heart muscle. Hawthorn and turmeric are herbs known to help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, thus alleviating angina symptoms. Hypertension, or high blood pressure, can also cause chest pain. Herbs such as garlic, hawthorn, and fish oil have been shown to help lower blood pressure, reducing the risk of chest pain and other cardiovascular issues. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
